home.h1: Your Ultimate Guide to the 2026 FIFA World Cup
home.intro: The 2026 FIFA World Cup is set to be the most expansive and thrilling tournament in history, jointly hosted by the United States, Canada, and Mexico. This guide helps you understand official facts, visualize all host venues on an interactive map, and plan smarter travel across time zones and cities.

facts.label: Venues
facts.value: 16 stadiums (11 in the USA, 3 in Mexico, 2 in Canada).

facts.label: Opening Match
facts.value: Estadio Azteca, Mexico City on June 11, 2026.

facts.label: The Final
facts.value: MetLife Stadium, New York/New Jersey on July 19, 2026.

hubs.title: Format Explained
hubs.desc: How the 48-team format works, from groups to the new Round of 32.

hubs.title: Host Cities & Venues
hubs.desc: Explore the 16 stadiums across the US, Canada, and Mexico.

hubs.title: Travel Planning
hubs.desc: Time zones, distances, flights, transit, and accommodation strategy.

travelGuide.metaTitle: 2026 World Cup Travel Guide | Plan Your Trip
travelGuide.intro: Because the tournament spans three countries and multiple time zones, travel logistics matter as much as match tickets. Use this guide to plan routes, minimize long-haul transfers, and pick accommodation strategically.

tips.label: Time Zones
tips.body: Matches will span Pacific, Mountain, Central, and Eastern time. Double-check kickoff times based on where you sleep.

tips.label: Weather & Seasons
tips.body: June–July is summer. Expect heat and humidity in some cities, and cooler evenings in others.

tips.label: Inter-City Travel
tips.body: Flights are often the most practical option between regions. Major hubs have frequent connections.

tips.label: Airports & Transit
tips.body: Plan match-day transit early and use public transit where possible to avoid traffic congestion.

tips.label: Accommodation
tips.body: Book early. Staying near transit lines often beats staying next to the stadium.

faq.q: Do I need a visa to travel between the US, Canada, and Mexico?
faq.a: Yes. Immigration rules apply separately for each country. Check visa/ESTA/eTA requirements based on your passport.

tournamentFormat.metaTitle: 2026 World Cup Tournament Format | 48 Teams Explained
tournamentFormat.intro: The 2026 World Cup expands to 48 teams. Here is how groups, qualification, and the knockout bracket work.

bullets.label: Group Stage
bullets.body: 48 teams are split into 12 groups of 4. Each team plays 3 group matches.

bullets.label: Progression
bullets.body: Top 2 in each group advance (24 teams). The 8 best third-place teams also advance (8 teams).

bullets.label: Knockout
bullets.body: A Round of 32 is added, then Round of 16, Quarter-finals, Semi-finals, and the Final.

faq.q: How many teams will play in the 2026 World Cup?
faq.a: 48 teams will participate for the first time.

faq.q: How many matches will each team play?
faq.a: Every team plays at least 3 matches. The Finalists will play 8 matches total.

faq.q: What is the new Round of 32?
faq.a: 32 teams advance from the group stage, so an extra knockout round is needed before the Round of 16.
Teams are seeded into pots based on FIFA rankings and drawn into 12 groups with geographic constraints.

venues.metaTitle: 2026 World Cup Host Venues | Official Guide
venues.introBody: The 2026 tournament spans three host nations and 16 stadiums. This page helps you understand where the venues are, why they matter, and how to plan travel around match locations.

faq.q: Which venue will host the Final?
faq.a: The Final will take place at MetLife Stadium in New York/New Jersey on July 19, 2026.
Estadio Azteca in Mexico City will host the opening match on June 11, 2026.

faq.q: How many matches will be played in each country?
faq.a: The US will host the majority of matches (78), while Canada and Mexico will host 13 matches each.

azteca.introBody: Estadio Azteca is one of football’s most iconic venues and will host the Opening Match on June 11, 2026. Its altitude and history make it a special destination for fans planning a World Cup trip.

faq.q: What is the capacity of Estadio Azteca?
faq.a: For the tournament it is expected to be around 87,523.

faq.q: How do I get to the stadium from downtown Mexico City?
faq.a: A common option is the Tren Ligero (light rail) to the Estadio Azteca station, connecting with the Metro system.
It is planned to be upgraded to meet FIFA requirements while preserving its historic identity.

metlife.metaTitle: MetLife Stadium | 2026 World Cup Final Venue
metlife.introBody: MetLife Stadium will host the 2026 World Cup Final on July 19, 2026. Its location near New York City and its large capacity make it a top venue for fans planning the ultimate match-day experience.

faq.q: When is the 2026 World Cup Final?
faq.a: The Final will be held on July 19, 2026 at MetLife Stadium.

faq.q: What is the capacity of MetLife Stadium?
Will matches be played on artificial turf?
faq.a: No. A temporary natural grass pitch will be installed to meet FIFA requirements.
